body:
We can stop the Left, but we need your help!

************

The Democrats want to destroy everything America stands for. We can stop them, but we need your help NOW.

Fellow Conservative, with your help, we can smash through our mid-month donation target. **We 're currently short by just 129 contributions from Top Trump Patriots. **

[**Can I count on you to rush in a last minute donation to fuel a grassroots surge and push us over the top?**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=76c7a182581b49be9978a42f569a18af&_e=xzDG_aUHDhyBn6sQB2-RhiT1c6sy3dNj4W-5j5WVgrl0ppQks7oKi_NgjzG004TkZvQnoNl9aSN76U5R5Hzj2DnblhJ0jyPPEfqUXt0wkBFGk1RrkPNNcBjNeG0hwZs_O0A3ppSFJEsMf00j4fhyxX9T0Hhvac8b9ej3BPm1tK1x2FyJzXjpYScePyrchrw_EYJ_GfotBO9KqQlVJiOx-QMIQ0cDLCH9U8Ybj6OQIaxFSrU_K8z1bUc3Gq18MEG0WJCf1hUF8Skh4Yb-YoGHrOVy9xvW_lt5tdmyahTG3_exJ_PyWa4AVsccCYH9dC9uRMzZf3I7jmt4JELY8A8A5SpJxARtCc44ceuTX1X2KDnNNRnK5huDEwLmFmg24InfqatAan2sCEhis6sy_4Fr1d8py4SzQ2SF5SnFn1oxgYjSSf-df2xGQKXoLu0D--LmqSO_-OIy_nm2ZLfBm-eCUgqNMwAsvhK69UiomEVb4QGhIMLEeUbpykwuNRCQQNUZqx9tawxF_WS-Qpf74kVvbg%3D%3D)Trump's teased that he'll run for President if we flip the House, but if we fall short of our goals, _the Radical Left will remain in TOTAL control. _

129 contributions may not seem like much, but hitting our goal is the only way to ensure we're on the path to victory. The Democrats are poised to lose everything, so we can't stop now.

**The Socialist Left has already stockpiled MILLIONS to attack us. It 's easy when the Left has Hollywood, San Francisco, and New York billionaires on their side. But we've got YOU on our side! With only 129 contributions needed to make our mark, [can I count on you to be the one to help us hit our goal? We only have 1 HOUR to catch up before we permanently fall behind. ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=76c7a182581b49be9978a42f569a18af&_e=xzDG_aUHDhyBn6sQB2-RhiT1c6sy3dNj4W-5j5WVgrl0ppQks7oKi_NgjzG004TkZvQnoNl9aSN76U5R5Hzj2DnblhJ0jyPPEfqUXt0wkBFGk1RrkPNNcBjNeG0hwZs_O0A3ppSFJEsMf00j4fhyxX9T0Hhvac8b9ej3BPm1tK1x2FyJzXjpYScePyrchrw_EYJ_GfotBO9KqQlVJiOx-QMIQ0cDLCH9U8Ybj6OQIaxFSrU_K8z1bUc3Gq18MEG0WJCf1hUF8Skh4Yb-YoGHrOVy9xvW_lt5tdmyahTG3_exJ_PyWa4AVsccCYH9dC9u4yHijuIjUG_39O06ZbV6ctUKMpyQAa5wx0unyA_AFiUoJTvY_AfIS8By_qohYp4v13S-Ubp0I9rPU7xjFqOr_wpFcbLg2mJ4IDrZwpZ1gYzE8sJ7WKGjJbyXiNC3WK-wIcE0lLJesx_954pG6pJ0TA3rQn9t48JpJiqkPmBzYQWPhr1g0I6O3839bLvU2X5ZCjo9iNpQ-Hp3dZ7oyfNXdw%3D%3D)**

Devin Nunes

name: Kevin Faulconer
subject: FWD: It’s about MORE than reopening
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Faulconer for Governor 2021
body:
FWD: Kevin Faulconer knows what California really needs to turn Newsom’s mess around. Read more >>
Peter, I hope you saw John’s email about my recent Fox San Francisco interview on California’s reopening. I’m forwarding it to you now just in case you missed it. I’ve said it before and I will say it again — fixing the Golden State is about more than just reopening. _**It’s about REBUILDING and REFORMING. **_ I’m ready for this election, and I’m ready to lead a California Comeback. But I need your help to spread my message. **Please RUSH a grassroots donation right now!** [**DONATE >>**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/55832bfcd7802031c3d6b838967d376c/34943) Together, we can end Newsom’s reign in California and make our state number one in the nation yet again. -Kevin Faulconer | |
---
To: All Supporters
From: John Burke
Subject: It’s about MORE than reopening
Date: Thursday, June 17, 2021 5:10 p.m.
[](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/95c26e91f98dc317c3d6b838967d376c/34943)
Friend,

California is officially “reopened,” but we still have a long way to go to get completely back on track.

After more than a year of Newsom’s unscientific lockdowns — not to mention more than a decade of one-party rule in Sacramento — it’s going to take more than a day for a real California Comeback.

Kevin Faulconer took a quick break from the campaign trail this week to talk with Heather Holmes of KTVU, and discuss what it’s really going to take to make California the land of Golden Opportunity once again.
[**< < CLICK HERE TO WATCH & SHARE >>**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/3d1cc3f5398356b5c3d6b838967d376c/34943)
Californians are clamoring for new leadership at the top — that’s why we were able to turn in over 2 million signatures on the recall petition this spring, and why the Recall Election _will happen_ in 2021.

Make sure that we elect a proven leader to replace Gavin Newsom this fall. We need to do more than just reopen. We need to rebuild and reform, and there is only one candidate in the race with a real plan and the experience necessary to make it happen.

Please help us share Kevin’s message of a California Comeback by **[sharing this video](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/dd1cedab195468c6c3d6b838967d376c/34943)** on Facebook or Twitter, or even just by forwarding this email to friends and family.

Thanks for your help spreading the word,
John Burke, Communications Director
| [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/03fd5ab504d4d8b2c3d6b838967d376c/34943) | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/7a9ee0087ad876bdc3d6b838967d376c/34943) | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/2f1a7ca5b8260137c3d6b838967d376c/34943) | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/b8d0d2556ccf170a3a89ac0c84d83476/34943/d103086132526e7d/89e0e0bc5df609c6c3d6b838967d376c/34943)

clean_body: FWD: Kevin Faulconer knows what California really needs to turn Newsom’s mess around. Read more >> Peter, I hope you saw John’s email about my recent Fox San Francisco interview on California’s reopening. I’m forwarding it to you now just in case you missed it. I’ve said it before and I will say it again — fixing the Golden State is about more than just reopening. It’s about REBUILDING and REFORMING. I’m ready for this election, and I’m ready to lead a California Comeback. But I need your help to spread my message. Please RUSH a grassroots donation right now! Together, we can end Newsom’s reign in California and make our state number one in the nation yet again. -Kevin Faulconer | | To: All Supporters From: John Burke Subject: It’s about MORE than reopening Date: Thursday, June 17, 2021 5:10 p.m. Friend, California is officially “reopened,” but we still have a long way to go to get completely back on track. After more than a year of Newsom’s unscientific lockdowns — not to mention more than a decade of one-party rule in Sacramento — it’s going to take more than a day for a real California Comeback. Kevin Faulconer took a quick break from the campaign trail this week to talk with Heather Holmes of KTVU, and discuss what it’s really going to take to make California the land of Golden Opportunity once again. Californians are clamoring for new leadership at the top — that’s why we were able to turn in over 2 million signatures on the recall petition this spring, and why the Recall Election will happen in 2021. Make sure that we elect a proven leader to replace Gavin Newsom this fall. We need to do more than just reopen. We need to rebuild and reform, and there is only one candidate in the race with a real plan and the experience necessary to make it happen. Please help us share Kevin’s message of a California Comeback by on Facebook or Twitter, or even just by forwarding this email to friends and family. Thanks for your help spreading the word, John Burke, Communications Director | | | | ---|---|---|--- id:2021-06-17-14:16:15:216t Paid for by Faulconer for Governor 2021

name: Susan Wild
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Susan Wild for Congress
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31047832/291436699/-563716641?refcode=BLU-SWC-20210619-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYzRhNTI3MC1mN2QwLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x6SRI5ShRande7VYH2vl7aqBx99WxVx8UpIXD-83cfg=)
---
| Peter,

Today is Juneteenth, a day to commemorate the emancipation of enslaved people in the United States. As we remember this historic moment, we must also grapple with the inequity and racism that persists in our country to this very day.

This past year has seen the United States and especially White Americans have frank conversations about racism in an unprecedented and much-needed way, as the number of unarmed Black Americans who are killed because of the color of their skin grows every day.

**As an elected official, and as a distraught citizen, I am committed to supporting our Black communities and finding solutions. If you, like me, want to take action to be a part of the solution,[_I encourage you to consider making a donation to the NAACP to support their fight for civil rights._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31047833/291436700/-1890789315?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYzRhNTI3MC1mN2QwLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x6SRI5ShRande7VYH2vl7aqBx99WxVx8UpIXD-83cfg=&emci=0182b544-4fd0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=fc4a5270-f7d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=5543981)**

We cannot turn a blind eye to this systemic problem. So long as Black Americans must walk the streets in fear of those very officials who are supposed to protect them, we are far from being a perfect Union. I will not rest until we see liberation from the fear that permeates Black communities. I hope you will join me in this effort.

In solidarity,
_Susan_
---

name: Jahana Hayes
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
Today marks the anniversary of independence for the millions of Black Americans who were held in the bondage of slavery. On this day in 1865, a Union general arrived in Galveston, Texas with news that slaves in the former rebel state were free. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31048483/291437841/-1295403277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZTkxZDkxNi1mYWQwLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4BMfs-Mt0iaZjJevy0kFlJKvUaKMGFYWBHtPJdJNRHg=&emci=da309625-7ccf-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=ce91d916-fad0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=15529135) | Today marks the anniversary of independence for the millions of Black Americans who were held in the bondage of slavery. On this day in 1865, a Union general arrived in Galveston, Texas with news that slaves in the former rebel state were free. This announcement came almost two and a half years after President Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation which officially outlawed slavery. These events gave birth to “Juneteenth,” the oldest commemoration of the abolishment of slavery in the United States and true independence for all Americans. That history is directly tied to my being elected as the first African American woman from Connecticut to the U.S. Congress. We must keep moving forward, because true freedom will not exist until we have all voices at the table, representing all people. Today, I am thinking of our ancestors who risked their lives for emancipation, for a change that would reshape generations to come. Today, I am reflecting on my vote in the House to make Juneteenth a federal holiday and my joining President Biden as he signed the bill into law. Today is a celebration of how far we have come. It is also a reminder of how much more we still have to do. – Jahana
---

name: Unite the Country
subject: What Juneteenth means.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Unite the Country, unitethecountry
body:
Unite the Country [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/EA/EA005/1/78987/images/utc-web-logo.png]
Peter -

On June 19, 1865, two and a half years after President Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation, Union soldiers landed in Galveston, Texas with news that the formerly enslaved were free, effectively ending slavery in the United States.

On Thursday, almost 156 years later, President Biden signed a bill establishing Juneteenth National Independence Day as an official federal holiday.

Juneteenth is a celebration of Black perseverance, achievement, and freedom. It is a powerful reminder of this country’s ability to change — to fight systemic racism, white supremacy, and hate. And together, we can continue to pave the road to real and lasting justice, so that we may finally become the extraordinary nation that was promised to all Americans.

As we celebrate today, we must acknowledge the sacrifices made by the brave African Americans who endured 400 years of suffering during slavery.

But this is also the legacy of perseverance in the face of oppression: the hallmark of the African American experience in the fight for equality.

Happy Juneteenth! [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/EA/EA005/1/78987/images/em_UTC_OTH_Juneteenth_061921_EM.png]

Making Juneteenth a federal holiday is just the first step in the fight to reconcile the atrocities of slavery with our fight for equality.

Because the fight to protect our Black and brown communities is far from over.

Together, we’re building a united, more equitable country.

Happy Juneteenth,

Unite the Country
Unite the Country was formed in 2019 to support Joe Biden’s candidacy for President. After a resounding victory in 2020, our goal now is to communicate to the American electorate how President Biden’s agenda will both advance broad and desperately needed policy changes and help to heal our fractured political environment.
Unite the Country is proud to continue to support President Biden’s bold agenda and vision for America, and we will support candidates for public office who have similar foresight and courage. Donate now to support President Joe Biden in the political and legislative fights ahead! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/utc_em?amount=other&refcode=em_utc_fr_email-footer-1]
Follow Unite the Country on YouTube [https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCSsSjUM2HmY_luwVlWC1UmA]
Follow Unite the Country on Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/UniteTheCountryPAC]
Follow Unite the Country on Twitter [https://twitter.com/UniteCountryPAC]
Follow Unite the Country on Instagram [https://www.instagram.com/unitethecountry/]

name: Sarah Godlewski
subject: Ways to get involved this Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SARAH FOR WISCONSIN
body:
On this Juneteenth, as we celebrate emancipation and remember those who fought for freedom, we need to recognize how far we have to go to reckon with our past and work to end the injustices slavery caused -- racial injustices that persist to this day.

From the ballot box to policing to education, systemic racism still exists today, and Black Americans are still fighting for equality and equity under the law. But we must stand together. No one fights alone.

It's on each and every one of us (particularly white folks!) to educate ourselves and get involved in the fight for racial justice. That's why I want to lift up organizations and opportunities to further the work:
* **Black Leaders Organizing for Communities (BLOC)** in Milwaukee works to lift up Black voices, invest in our communities, and advocate for real change. Learn more about BLOC and how to get involved **[here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31054354/291460124/1314615717?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTYyOThhOC0wNmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3-tnC8NxLYez08D-_D7VhUHCseEUYsPk3OBzkqgzqkA=&emci=cb6c6fad-b1cf-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=c16298a8-06d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=18659593) **
* The **Kujichagulia Madison Center for Self-Determination** is organizing many Juneteenth activities, and you can find ways to support their work **[here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31054355/291460153/1315104929?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTYyOThhOC0wNmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3-tnC8NxLYez08D-_D7VhUHCseEUYsPk3OBzkqgzqkA=&emci=cb6c6fad-b1cf-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=c16298a8-06d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=18659593)**
* **100 Black Men of Madison** and **100 Black Men of Milwaukee** do incredible work to foster the next generation of Wisconsin talent. You can learn more about their work and support them**[here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31054356/291460154/-1989876437?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTYyOThhOC0wNmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3-tnC8NxLYez08D-_D7VhUHCseEUYsPk3OBzkqgzqkA=&emci=cb6c6fad-b1cf-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=c16298a8-06d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=18659593)** and**[here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31054359/291460156/-1800096574?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTYyOThhOC0wNmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3-tnC8NxLYez08D-_D7VhUHCseEUYsPk3OBzkqgzqkA=&emci=cb6c6fad-b1cf-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=c16298a8-06d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=18659593) **
* The **NAACP Wisconsin State Conference** works with local chapters to help advocate for the well-being and equality of Black Wisconsinites. Read more about their work and support them **[here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31054360/291460158/1046467662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTYyOThhOC0wNmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3-tnC8NxLYez08D-_D7VhUHCseEUYsPk3OBzkqgzqkA=&emci=cb6c6fad-b1cf-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=c16298a8-06d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=18659593) **

Today, I hope you'll take the time to reflect on the enormity of the work we have left to do and fight on.

Thank you,

Sarah

name: Sharif Street
subject: The fight for justice isn’t over
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARIF STREET FOR PA
body:
When slavery ended ... Jim Crow began
Today is Juneteenth, a holiday celebrating the freedom of the last enslaved people in the south in 1865, two months after the Confederate army surrendered, in which Union soldiers came to the island of Galveston, Texas and enforced the emancipation of all enslaved people.
However, while the holiday is celebrated as the end of slavery, that’s not exactly true. Union states like Kentucky kept African folks enslaved until the ratification of the 13th Amendment.
Thereafter, Black folks and former African slaves faced Jim Crow, a racist prison industrial complex, segregated schools that cripled their educational attainment, and so much more.
**Now, Republicans are continuing that despicable part of American history by trying to justify disenfranchising people who look like me. **
In Pennsylvania, we’re seeing an effort put forth by the former president and his allies to undermine the integrity of Pennsylvania’s elections and pass voting suppression laws that are little more than the re-enactment of those very same Jim Crow policies.
_It’s the same story, again and again. _
But we can break the cycle. Together, we can fight back against systemic racism and white supremacy in our country and our Commonwealth.
If elected, I would be only the 10th Black person to ever hold a seat in the U.S. Senate, and would be the first Muslim ever elected to the Senate.
This means more than just history making -- it will show to Black and brown people and children across the country that we can overcome these racist systems and that we can fix them for future generations.
[_**If you’re with me, consider making a $6.19 donation in commemoration of the progress we’ve made and how far we still have yet to go.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31056225/291466597/-1735875613?refcode=E9229-20210619-Juneteenth-SL-RacialJustice-PlainText-NA-NA&amount=6.19&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84MzEwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjA5ZjE5ZS0wOGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N0qxc9avx5Wr5zhqAYXI8PgL_fmMNkUp7Wyz38o7kzg=)
Yours,
Sharif Street

PAID FOR BY SHARIF STREET FOR PA

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/35af6cc4-d4ce-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7/bf09f19e-08d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84MzEwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjA5ZjE5ZS0wOGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N0qxc9avx5Wr5zhqAYXI8PgL_fmMNkUp7Wyz38o7kzg=&id=117874703).

body:
The truth is that today, over a century after the emancipation on June 19, 1865, Black Americans are still fighting for justice. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31060683/291470691/300550190?refcode=EM_FR_210619jt_N_AL_90DA_7_NG_N_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODcxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODgwMWFhNy0wZGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BIDxONttEHKiptZTaY4LKAT9m9Unj3BhovlVhuqtHX8=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31060684/291470692/300550190?refcode=EM_FR_210619jt_N_AL_90DA_7_NG_N_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODcxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODgwMWFhNy0wZGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BIDxONttEHKiptZTaY4LKAT9m9Unj3BhovlVhuqtHX8=) Peter,

Today is Juneteenth -- the anniversary of Union troops marching to free 250,000 enslaved people at the end of the civil war.

Juneteenth is widely recognized as a celebration of Black independence and the resilience of the Black community. **But the truth is that today, over a century after the emancipation on June 19, 1865, Black Americans are still fighting for justice.**

I’m excited to spend this Saturday celebrating the 50th Anniversary of Milwaukee’s Juneteenth Day, one of the county’s oldest running Juneteenth celebrations.

**[But Juneteenth isn’t just a celebration, it’s a reminder that we still have work to do. I’m asking you to split a donation of $10, $25, or whatever you can to support the work of the NAACP, Southern Poverty Law Center, and Black Lives Matter Network Action.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31060685/291470693/300550190?refcode=EM_FR_210619jt_N_AL_90DA_7_NG_N_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODcxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODgwMWFhNy0wZGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BIDxONttEHKiptZTaY4LKAT9m9Unj3BhovlVhuqtHX8=)** **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31060686/291470694/300550190?refcode=EM_FR_210619jt_N_AL_90DA_7_NG_N_b1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODcxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODgwMWFhNy0wZGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BIDxONttEHKiptZTaY4LKAT9m9Unj3BhovlVhuqtHX8=)** What happened last summer to George Floyd, Breonna Taylor, Jacob Blake and countless other Black people across our country shines a painful light on systemic racism and police brutality that we still deal with today.

**We can’t just keep talking about the problems, we have to do something about it.** That’s why I’m running for Senate, and that’s why I’m asking you to take action today by splitting a contribution between the NAACP, Southern Poverty Law Center, and Black Lives Matter Network Action.

**[Help us raise $10,000 by midnight tonight for organizations leading the way to combat racial injustice>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31060687/291470695/300550190?refcode=EM_FR_210619jt_N_AL_90DA_7_NG_N_L2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODcxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODgwMWFhNy0wZGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BIDxONttEHKiptZTaY4LKAT9m9Unj3BhovlVhuqtHX8=)**

Thank you,

Alex Lasry ** **

I’m excited to spend this Saturday celebrating the 50th Anniversary of Milwaukee’s Juneteenth Day, one of the county’s oldest running Juneteenth celebrations. = = What happened last summer to George Floyd, Breonna Taylor, Jacob Blake and countless other Black people across our country shines a painful light on systemic racism and police brutality that we still deal with today. We can’t just keep talking about the problems, we have to do something about it. That’s why I’m running for Senate, and that’s why I’m asking you to take action today by splitting a contribution between the NAACP, Southern Poverty Law Center, and Black Lives Matter Network Action. = Thank you, Alex Lasry Paid for by Lasry for Wisconsin Stay connected with Alex's campaign to defeat Ron Johnson: Lasry for Wisconsin P.O. Box 511549 Milwaukee, WI 53203 United States It's going to take everyone on this team to win this election, but if you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . If you'd like to stay on our list but receive fewer emails, fill out .

body:
I wanted to let you know President Trump wanted to personally thank all of the incredible supporters of Team Cammack.

| Friend,

It was great to spend some time with 45 the other day. He knows I have and will continue to fight for an America First Agenda in Congress. Like President Trump, I went to Washington to get to work and save our country.

| As you know, my job is far from over. Every day I am working hard to expose the radical policies coming from Washington D.C., which is why I ask that you [continue your support for the America First Agenda.](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ry4iJ-I-aadtHAjtOOGG0yjVHZa897hHIJIMArZIhlJ8Lq0MBQ8W51ld6DtvFH2l7QraijCbXRdssfGvh4Y76ganZRVYp1lrOhab6-sStCF2F1MYvwRfp7S6rAt2T1wdha_WXN04MmI81tWTWKN-I1SfA4kTGYWy_EvFydG-AhMO_syZYESfb2vMkvLUyD5C&c=p4WacRyPVb9LWc2yqxE2QjhZwdACuZR8dqkeqgK-N8v5JR6JY_HZzQ==&ch=K6QR4gdDdeHfvRFWM7YdBdSnOvSQXty3JD4U6Qcpfc0rPUnrQBw2kQ==) It is important to me to represent the great state of Florida, and the progress that we have made all across the country.

name: Mondaire Jones
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_
body:
#

Today, we celebrate Juneteenth, a holiday that marks a major turning point in the fight for Black emancipation in the era of slavery. I’d like to take a moment to explain the significance of Juneteenth, why I voted this week to make it a national holiday, and why it’s so important that we continue to recognize and honor this moment in history.
As many of you know, the Emancipation Proclamation, which was passed on January 1, 1863, legally established the freedom of all enslaved people in Confederate States.
**Despite this law, however, hundreds of thousands of people who lived in Confederate-occupied areas remained enslaved — until June 19, 1865, two months after the end of the Civil War. On this day, Union troops reached Galveston, Texas, and notified the last people enslaved in the United States that they were free.**
Today, and every year, Juneteenth is a reminder of the atrocities that our nation inflicted upon Black people during the era of slavery, the joy and promise felt by the newly freed on that day, and the battles still to come — 156 years later — in the fight for equality in our country.
I recognize, as a history-making Black man, that my role as a member of Congress is, in some ways, representative of how far we’ve come.
But structural barriers to freedom and equality persist. Last summer, protests broke out after the murders of George Floyd and Breonna Taylor at the hands of police officers. Black people are disproportionately victimized by the police and imprisoned, including for non-violent offenses. Over 40% of inmates on death row are Black.
And many of my Republican colleagues in the House will release statements today honoring Juneteenth while supporting legislation that makes it harder for Black people to vote.
When Congressional Republicans vote against legislation to expand voting rights and grant statehood to DC, and when they vote to preserve the Jim Crow filibuster, they’re signaling support for the legacy of Jim Crow.
It is my duty, as a member of Congress, to fight back against these attempts to turn back the clock on civil rights. Staying vigilant is the only way we can protect the progress we’ve made.
So today, I am choosing not only to remember what happened on June 19, 1865, but to commit to upholding freedom and equality for all in our country — in honor of all who were freed on that day, and in honor of everyone who has put their foot down in the name of racial justice ever since.
Thank you, as always, for your support. Together, we will win this fight.
Onward,
Mondaire
* * *

name: Hendrell Remus
subject: TNDP's New Website and Merch Store 🔥
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, CAROL V
body:
https://tndp.org/ [https://tndp.org/]
Peter,
Your Tennessee Democratic Party has a new website [https://tndp.org/] and we wanted you to be the first to give it a look. Completely revamped with a fresh new design that's packed with resources for voters, volunteers, and candidates alike — all so you can easily get plugged into Democratic organizing in Tennessee.
Oh, and we also have a brand new merch store [https://shop.tndp.org/] so you can rep some TNDP swag with plenty of rainbows for Pride.
https://shop.tndp.org/ [https://shop.tndp.org/]
Give our new site a tour at tndp.org [https://tndp.org/] . If you want to go straight to the good stuff at our merch store click here [https://shop.tndp.org/] .
We hope you not only enjoy the new website, but that you utilize it as a powerful tool for voter mobilization so that we can Build Back Blue together.
Thanks for being a Tennessee Democrat,
Hendrell Remus
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

subject: Fox News just admitted:

name: Malcolm Kenyatta
subject: Emancipation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Malcolm PAC
body:
#

In 1862, President Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation and declared an end to the vile institution of slavery in the United States.
Most enslaved people were freed, but many were held captive even after the proclamation was signed.
It wasn't until two and a half years later, on June 19, 1865, that the word of freedom finally reached the remaining enslaved people in Galveston, Texas.
That day, the day when the last enslaved people in America were freed, is what we celebrate today.
And on this Juneteenth, I'm taking time to remember and honor the people, my ancestors, who fought for the progress we've made and reflect on the work we still must do.
We all know the ugly truth of what followed emancipation. Jim Crow. Mass incarceration. Red-lining.
Black Americans have continued to face barrier after barrier. Systemic racism remains so deeply rooted in our laws and institutions.
But as long as good hardworking people like you and me remain committed to justice for all of us — no matter where we live, how we look, or who we love — we can have hope.
That means recommitting ourselves to our fight for accountability in policing, reforming our criminal justice system, and addressing racial disparities in health care, wealth, and education.
And, most importantly, it means recommitting ourselves to our fight for real justice and equality for all.
I hope you'll join me.
Thank you,
Malcolm
Malcolm Kenyatta is running to be Pennsylvania's next United States Senator.
If elected, he will be the first Black American to represent Pennsylvania in the Senate.
[Will you make a contribution to support his historic candidacy?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31063063/291482161/-347207331?refcode2=eb0d5a5b-14d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&refcode=em210619-1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODc1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjBkNWE1Yi0xNGQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zpUnn6DouiLE0IIOa_vXJsr9lbCYMShz_su9kTDU4Ac=)

name: Dr. Kim Schrier
subject: Happy Juneteenth!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
In 1863, President Abraham Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation, marking the beginning of the end of slavery in the United States. However, it wasn't until June 19, 1865, months after the end of the Civil War, that this news finally reached the enslaved Black people of Galveston, Texas and the last slaves learned they were free. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31063585/291485960/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OGM2ZTE2Yi0xN2QxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LUE23snC_8rkPJ5IGvs9ZVi2tCOAAl_Y4LfCHUB9v9U=&emci=16504f0e-45d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=78c6e16b-17d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

In 1863, President Abraham Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation, marking the beginning of the end of slavery in the United States. However, it wasn't until June 19, 1865, months after the end of the Civil War, that this news finally reached the enslaved Black people of Galveston, Texas and the last slaves learned they were free.

We celebrate Juneteenth to commemorate the ending of slavery in the United States, and to honor the memory of enslaved Black Americans and their resiliency. While this day is a tribute to how far we’ve come, it also serves as a reminder of how far we still have to go for full lived equality. I’m proud to have voted this week in Congress to make Juneteenth a federal holiday, so we can continue to celebrate and remember this day.

Nearly 160 years after the Emancipation Proclamation, we're still working to heal the deep wounds and systemic problems that are a consequence of 400 years of slavery, racism, and inequality on our soil. In our criminal justice system, schools, hospitals, and economy, Black Americans face discrimination and inequity every day.

Over the last year, people from across the country have united their voices in the call for justice and equity. These protests became one of the largest movements in American history, but we have a ways to go to live up to the promise of equality made in our Constitution.

As we celebrate Juneteenth today, and commemorate the end of a dark period in our nation's history, I hope you will join me in recommitting to the fight for equality.

Thank you,

Kim

| Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
---

Issaquah, WA 98027

That's exactly what I want to ask Senator Chuck Schumer and the Democrats when it comes to their ridiculous efforts to control our elections.

---

What makes America so special is our trust in the ability to speak our minds at the polls and elect who we want. But with the corrupt election law bills recently introduced in Congress, the Left is **TAKING THAT AWAY FROM YOU. **

They're not just hurting today's political balance, they're destroying the very democratic measures we've fought to protect since America's founding. [**You have to help me stop them.**](http://ct4.americasnewsletter.com/t/10048721/224717773/203443/5/?ff2f3d13=cnAuMTAxNDk5&ff2f3d13=cnAuMTAxNDk5&x=95e3641e)

This is a fight we **CANNOT** fail. It is imperative that you join me in this fight.

Tim Scott

name: Mike Collier
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Collier Committee
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/89772/images/Logo%20%282%29.png]
Hello team,

Nearly 156 years ago, Union and U.S.C.T. troops marched into Galveston, Texas, ordering all slaves to be free, two years after Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation in 1863.

Although delayed, about 250,000 enslaved Black Texans were granted freedom and liberation on June 19, 1865 — a day where we now celebrate the emancipation of slavery all across the nation called Juneteenth.

From then on, Galveston became a symbol of resilience with annual celebrations, community gatherings, and pilgrimages to the city for generations. In 1979, Texas was the first state to declare Juneteenth a holiday.

While we honor the patriots who came before us, we are also reminded that the road to justice is long, and the fight for freedom and equality continues today.

Systemic racism and racial discrimination are embedded in our nation’s institutions, and we have a responsibility to right the wrongs of the past to fulfill the promise of a more just future.

For decades, lawmakers have been slowly chipping away at voting rights to disenfranchise Black and brown voters from exercising their right to vote. Last month, the Texas GOP tried ramming through SB 7 — one of the strictest voting bills in the country since Jim Crow.

Thanks to activists and leaders stepping up, it didn't pass. But we can bet they’ll try again and again.

We’ve come too far to let politicians like Dan Patrick and Greg Abbott take away our voices. The right to vote is sacred, and now more than ever, we all must renew our commitment to protecting voter rights for all Texans.

I believe that we can lead the way on voting rights and criminal justice reform, just like we led the way in honoring Juneteenth as a holiday. At a time when our state leaders are trying to ban civil rights education in schools, we cannot allow them to silence our voices.

So today, we celebrate the progress we've made, but tomorrow we’ll continue the fight for racial justice and absolute equality.

Happy Juneteenth,

— Mike
The Collier Committee
P.O. Box 14648
Humble, TX 77347
United States
[https://collierfortexas.com/] |
Paid for by the Collier Committee

However, if you’d still like to receive fewer emails, fill out this form here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2abP63YYcUqXP5eHRkxWIQ2]. If you wish to no longer receive these messages, please unsubscribe. subject: re: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tony Cárdenas For Congress P
body:
on this day 156 years ago:

| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31066338/291497875/-1119657662?refcode=header-img&amount=25&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTE2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODc4ODdkMC0xZmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=obdoP_ZZMTytRJ3s2WZNCsWYcOE6nUvx0biGZu5BKIE=)
---
Peter,

156 years ago today, the United States liberated the last people who had been enslaved.

Two whole years after President Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation.

**This long overlooked moment in American history will now be recognized across the country.** I am proud to have cosponsored legislation to make Juneteenth National Independence Day a federal holiday.

Peter, I hope this day will serve as an important reminder of what we have accomplished and what we must continue to work towards.

It is a reminder that even 156 years later, people of color in this country are still reeling from systemic racism in our own sacred institutions.

It is a reminder that we must fight back against racially targeted policies, including gross voter suppression laws designed to suppress the votes of Black and Brown Americans.

It is a reminder that we must keep paving the path for true equality for all.

**We have made great progress, but this Juneteenth, I hope you’ll join me in reflecting on what we can do to advance racial equity going forward.**

In solidarity,

Tony Cárdenas
| | Paid for by Tony Cárdenas For Congress P.O. Box 15320, Washington, DC 20003
www.tonycardenas.com

name: Karen Bass
subject: Fighting Back To Know Our History
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Karen Bass for Congress
body:
Friend,

As I'm sure you've seen in the news lately, Republicans across the country are trying their hardest to erase and ignore the involvement of race in our country's founding and in our country's existence.

This week, we took an important step in fighting against their attempts to sanitize this country's history.

The truth is, the legacy of slavery lives on and has shaped, in part, the world we live in today. Part of addressing these impacts is understanding the honest history of this country, which is what makes today so important. **It 's why, this week, I stood in the United States Capitol, which was built by enslaved African Americans, and voted to make Juneteenth a national holiday.**

But even though most of my colleagues voted for this bill, some still weren't on board -- one of my colleagues said acknowledging our country's history was us trying to "celebrate identity politics as part of its larger efforts to make Critical Race Theory the reigning ideology of our country."

Know this: we will not be able to fully address systemic injustices in this country until we are able to acknowledge the true history of this country. I will not stop in this fight -- and I hope you join me.

I'll see out there,

Karen

name: Derek Kilmer
subject: Remember, Celebrate, and Recommit
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer |
body:
Peter,

Today is Juneteenth, which marks the anniversary of when enslaved people in Galveston, Texas were finally granted their freedom -- two years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed.

We observe Juneteenth to celebrate freedom and emancipation, to remember our country's history of enslavement and discrimination, to honor the Black community, and to recommit to the fight for freedom and justice for all.

While we've made progress these past 156 years, we're continuing the important work of rooting out systemic racism and addressing the many inequities facing Black Americans.

Creating a world where true justice is real for Black Americans will not come from one jury verdict or the results of one election. It will require systemic change and countless meaningful reforms.

This work will be difficult -- but it will also be critical. That's why I was proud to be an original co-sponsor of the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act. It's why I'll continue to push for reform and accountability within our criminal justice system. It's why I'm pushing to address inequities in health care and education. It's why I'm working to protect voting rights. And it's why I'm fighting for economic opportunity for all Americans.

No one is free until we're all free. Together, we must continue demanding change to address systemic inequities and ensure justice for all.

It's an honor to serve you,

Derek Kilmer

---------------------------------------------
| Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer |
---------------------------------------------

People for Derek Kilmer
PO Box 1381
Tacoma WA 98402 United States

name: Phil Arballo
subject: this Juneteenth:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Arballo For Congress P
body:
| On this day 156 years ago -- more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed into law -- the last enslaved people in the United States learned they were finally free.

Today is a day to commemorate the freedom of our Black and Brown Americans, and this year, it takes on a magnified meaning, as our government has made Juneteenth a national holiday going forward.

While we should celebrate this day, we also must acknowledge continued injustices our country faces:
* Voter roll purges still overwhelmingly impact Black and Brown communities.

* People of color are still the targets of unfair treatment in many of our institutions, including in criminal justice and in medicine.

* Republican politicians are all-too-willing to use race-baiting language to divide us and discriminate against our marginalized communities.

Juneteenth is a celebration of the strength and resilience of Black and Brown communities in this country, but celebration is not enough. We’ve got a long way to go, and we must keep fighting for justice and true equality.

Thank you,

Phil
---
| | Paid for by Arballo For Congress P.O. Box 3406, Fresno, CA 93650
www.philarballo.com

clean_body: | On this day 156 years ago -- more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed into law -- the last enslaved people in the United States learned they were finally free. Today is a day to commemorate the freedom of our Black and Brown Americans, and this year, it takes on a magnified meaning, as our government has made Juneteenth a national holiday going forward. While we should celebrate this day, we also must acknowledge continued injustices our country faces: Voter roll purges still overwhelmingly impact Black and Brown communities. People of color are still the targets of unfair treatment in many of our institutions, including in criminal justice and in medicine. Republican politicians are all-too-willing to use race-baiting language to divide us and discriminate against our marginalized communities. Juneteenth is a celebration of the strength and resilience of Black and Brown communities in this country, but celebration is not enough. We’ve got a long way to go, and we must keep fighting for justice and true equality. Thank you, Phil | | Paid for by Arballo For Congress P.O. Box 3406, Fresno, CA 93650 www.philarballo.com Phil Arballo is a Latino, a Central Valley native, a father, and a husband. He's running for Congress in California's 22nd District against Devin Nunes -- a far-right, MAGA Republican who has turned his back on Central Valley families for two decades. Phil is a champion for real working families and will fight to protect health care access, keep guns out of the wrong hands, and stand up to traitorous Republicans who are working to ruin our Democracy. One more thing: Devin Nunes is terrified of losing to us. We came so close in 2020, even though Nunes outspent our campaign four-to-one. Make no mistake: Devin Nunes is vulnerable, but we need every good Democrat to step up and do their part to beat him. Please, consider making a =. name: Erica Smith
subject: Slavery was systemic, comprehensive, and generational — and its aftermath remains a crisis, one that requires a solution every bit as comprehensive and systemic.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Erica Smith for US Senate 2022
body:
Today, we celebrate Black resilience and Black joy — and reflect on the enormity of the intentional work we must do to dismantle the legacy of slavery.
#
On June 19th, 1865 — two and a half years after President Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation — the word of freedom finally reached enslaved people in Galveston, Texas.
That day today would come to be known and celebrated as Juneteenth. Today, we celebrate Black resilience and Black joy — and reflect on the enormity of the intentional work we must do to dismantle the legacy of slavery.
**Slavery was systemic, comprehensive, and generational — and its aftermath remains a crisis, one that requires a solution every bit as comprehensive and systemic.**
_One President is not enough. One day is not enough. One ruling is not enough._
**We need reparations.**
Reparations that invest in and build generational wealth in Black communities and provide capital for Black-owned businesses.
Reparations that rebuild our schools and ensure quality pre-K through college education for all children.
Reparations that end the prohibition of marijuana and fundamentally transform our criminal justice system to ensure that so many of our brothers and sisters aren’t ending up behind bars.
And yes, direct cash payments to Black Americans.
**It’s past time for our country to account for the intergenerational trauma inflicted by slavery, and its ongoing role in perpetuating and upholding white supremacy.[Will you add your name to join us in the fight to make reparations a reality?](https://act.ericaforus.com/signup/em_ms_ES_20210601_EA_reparations/?source=em20210619-190&t=1&akid=190%2E3698%2El_MJCS)**
**[Add your name »](https://act.ericaforus.com/signup/em_ms_ES_20210601_EA_reparations/?source=em20210619-190&t=2&akid=190%2E3698%2El_MJCS)**
We deserve to partake in the wealth that we helped create. We deserve opportunity, prosperity, and at long last: _full freedom._
With love and in solidarity,
Erica
Erica Smith for U.S. Senate

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/es-emails?refcode=em20210619-190-footer&t=3&refcode2=190_3698_l_MJCS&akid=190%2E3698%2El_MJCS)**
---
Email us: [info@ericaforus.com](mailto: info@ericaforus.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Grassroots donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Erica for Us, P.O. Box 1236, Gaston, NC 27832.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ericaforus.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ericaforus.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=4&akid=190%2E3698%2El_MJCS)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ericaforus.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=190%2E3698%2El_MJCS)

name: Team Heinrich
subject: Sign the petition: The Senate must act on climate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31068080/291503580/-272948131?refcode=MTH_em_20210619_X_ENG_CA_X_ACT_US_all_all_CA_TH_v1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZDRmZGE1Mi0yNWQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_zSkNzXHcOnYLVZ6p0ehw19w5y-E86hDnhBg26kii_Q=&emci=cc4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=cd4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=252045)
---
Here’s Martin’s stance: If an infrastructure package doesn’t include meaningful action on climate change, it shouldn’t count on every Democratic vote. Here’s why: **Climate change is the greatest, most existential crisis we face.** With this infrastructure package, we have a once-in-a-generation opportunity to take action on climate and create millions of jobs. To not seize this moment would be a disservice to our children and all future generations. [**If you agree that the Senate must approve an infrastructure package that addresses climate change like the crisis it is, add your name:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31068081/291503581/-677751503?refcode=MTH_em_20210619_X_ENG_CA_X_ACT_US_all_all_CA_TH_v1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZDRmZGE1Mi0yNWQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_zSkNzXHcOnYLVZ6p0ehw19w5y-E86hDnhBg26kii_Q=&emci=cc4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=cd4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=252045) | [SIGN THE PETITION →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31068082/291503582/-677751503?refcode=MTH_em_20210619_X_ENG_CA_X_ACT_US_all_all_CA_TH_v1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZDRmZGE1Mi0yNWQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_zSkNzXHcOnYLVZ6p0ehw19w5y-E86hDnhBg26kii_Q=&emci=cc4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=cd4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=252045)
---
We have the opportunity to move New Mexico and our country forward by establishing a clean energy standard to move toward 100% carbon-pollution free power and building a more resilient, reliable, 21st-century electric grid.
We can address climate change in an immediate, actionable way that puts millions of people to work by investing in the widespread electrification of appliances and machines in American homes and businesses.
And we must do everything we can to preserve our public lands and waters for future generations by protecting the Arctic Refuge from oil and gas drilling.
There’s a measure Martin has led on in the Senate that needs to also be included: mobilizing the next generation of conservation workers through the new Civilian Climate Corps to repair landscapes, grow our outdoor economy, and strengthen our resilience to climate change.
These are bold, substantial climate provisions. They shouldn’t be ignored in favor of a watered-down infrastructure package that doesn’t treat the climate crisis with the urgency it requires.
[**If you agree, add your name next to Martin’s. Call on the Senate to bring forward an infrastructure package that acts urgently on climate.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31068083/291503583/-677751503?refcode=MTH_em_20210619_X_ENG_CA_X_ACT_US_all_all_CA_TH_v1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZDRmZGE1Mi0yNWQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_zSkNzXHcOnYLVZ6p0ehw19w5y-E86hDnhBg26kii_Q=&emci=cc4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=cd4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=252045)
[SIGN THE PETITION →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31068084/291503584/-677751503?refcode=MTH_em_20210619_X_ENG_CA_X_ACT_US_all_all_CA_TH_v1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZDRmZGE1Mi0yNWQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_zSkNzXHcOnYLVZ6p0ehw19w5y-E86hDnhBg26kii_Q=&emci=cc4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=cd4fda52-25d1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=252045)

clean_body: Here’s Martin’s stance: If an infrastructure package doesn’t include meaningful action on climate change, it shouldn’t count on every Democratic vote. Here’s why: Climate change is the greatest, most existential crisis we face. With this infrastructure package, we have a once-in-a-generation opportunity to take action on climate and create millions of jobs. To not seize this moment would be a disservice to our children and all future generations. | We have the opportunity to move New Mexico and our country forward by establishing a clean energy standard to move toward 100% carbon-pollution free power and building a more resilient, reliable, 21st-century electric grid. We can address climate change in an immediate, actionable way that puts millions of people to work by investing in the widespread electrification of appliances and machines in American homes and businesses. And we must do everything we can to preserve our public lands and waters for future generations by protecting the Arctic Refuge from oil and gas drilling. There’s a measure Martin has led on in the Senate that needs to also be included: mobilizing the next generation of conservation workers through the new Civilian Climate Corps to repair landscapes, grow our outdoor economy, and strengthen our resilience to climate change. These are bold, substantial climate provisions. They shouldn’t be ignored in favor of a watered-down infrastructure package that doesn’t treat the climate crisis with the urgency it requires. Thanks for speaking out. —Team Heinrich | Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate Senator Martin Heinrich is committed to helping New Mexico become a leader in defense, tech, and energy with opportunities in every corner. This week's updates from Joe Kent (Candidate for WA-03) | | | |
---
| | |

|
####
# **Joe Kent
Highlights 13-19 June 2021**
---
| This week [Joe's interview from Tucker Carlson Today aired on Fox Nation on Friday 18 June 2021](https://joekentforcongress.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=520f0c556a8895811473126aa&id=64127a36a5&e=00856e661d). Joe was honored to have this opportunity to share his story, in-depth perspective on foreign policy, and views on endless wars. Referencing the interview, Tucker described his time with Joe Kent as, [“One of the most interesting conversations we’ve had in a long time. He’s an amazing person.”](https://joekentforcongress.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=520f0c556a8895811473126aa&id=98376c7a5e&e=00856e661d) If you missed it, [Episode 31, "Not Your Average Joe" is available here.](https://joekentforcongress.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=520f0c556a8895811473126aa&id=531edc77b8&e=00856e661d)

This week, Joe also surpassed the 200 volunteer mark. Joe is thrilled to have such motivated and dedicated volunteers and truly appreciates their support. To recognize their efforts over the last 4 months, he held a special Volunteer Appreciation event to recognize the people who are supporting this grassroots campaign from the ground up. Thank you, Volunteers!

This coming weekend are two special fundraising events, we would love to see you there if you're able to join us! Details are included below. Thanks everyone for a great week!

Sincerely,
Byron Sanford
**Saturday, 26 June: Black Tie Fundraiser at City Farm Chehalis 6-8pm** , Click link for details and tickets **[26 June Event Tickets](https://joekentforcongress.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=520f0c556a8895811473126aa&id=18fa4ae316&e=00856e661d)

body:
#

Friends - Let’s talk numbers.
**100** \- It’s been 100 days since the House passed H.R. 8, the Bipartisan Background Checks Act of 2021.
**54** \- In 2021, at least 54 Americans were shot and killed each day in gun assaults and homicides. That’s 8,100 people as of May.
**$18** \- Our average donation this month, and the average amount people are chipping in to save lives.
[**So here’s the plan, friend: Can you send a $3 donation today to help us urge the Senate to pass universal background checks? Your support, along with others, will help us pass this bill and save lives.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/giffords-em-jun21?amount=3&refcode=em210619-1-subs&t=1&refcode2=240_3935704_-X0379&akid=240%2E3935704%2E-X0379)

With Senate Democrats in control and the far-Left mob running amuck, our Majority Coalition members are the frontline in the battle to TAKE BACK the Senate Majority. Our values and our nation are on the line.

subject: No more Conspiracy theorists in congress!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BRENT HENNRICH FOR WASHINGTON](https://BH4C
body:
Jaime Herrera Beutler Vs the GOP ? | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/539f68ad4db4/flipwa03?e=9a7df6c10f)
---
[](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=6e3938d3f1&e=9a7df6c10f)
### Let's Heal Washington!
**Hello Peter ,**
**Things are heating up!** I am the Democratic Candidate for Washington’s third District, and I need your help.Since she stood up and voted to impeach #45 for his role in the “Big Lie” that ultimately culminated in the riotous insurrection of January 6th, 2021, Jaime Herrera Beutler has been besieged by opponents from the furthest reaches of _her own side of the aisle._ In an apparent “If you can’t beat them, join them” move _,_ she herself has begun to embrace the lies spread to stoke fear in her base _._ It is becoming apparent with Washington’s “Top Two” primary, we **must** address those who’s platforms are built on lies, fear, and conspiracies.
[](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=1eaff44722&e=9a7df6c10f)
Her most vocal and outspoken GOP opponent is US Army veteran Joe Kent. While I thank him for his years of service, my appreciation of him ends there. He has publicly stated his wishes to abolish the Department of Education, to reinstate the Trump era Muslim travel ban, and to **completely eliminate** any and all Pro-choice options for women.He is openly supported by some GOP “#AmericaFirst All-Stars like the Q-anon loving Marjorie Taylor Greene (Q-GA), and the “currently under DOJ investigation for sex trafficking of minors” Matt Gaetz (Q-FL)As you may already know, Washington is a “Top Two” State. That means if we don not have the support now to get the message out to the voters of my district we could very well end up with two Republican candidates on the general election ballot come November 2022. The voting system in Washington does not allow for a write-in option in the general election.With Red States gaining more congressional seats, Blue states losing many, and redistricting lines being gerrymandered by partisan commissions, this seat could very well determine what party controls the House majority in the next Congress. **I ask you to join me in keeping America’s Democracy intact! Please help me get the word out. Early support will make a large difference in this race, so tell your friends and neighbors, and if you can, please donate! Together we can**[**#FlipWA03**](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=25e7142bf8&e=9a7df6c10f)**You can support my run now by donating**[**BH4C.win.**](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=77df598a74&e=9a7df6c10f)**You can learn more about me by visiting**[**BH4C.com**](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=278c245f96&e=9a7df6c10f)
| | | | [](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=06a614e282&e=9a7df6c10f)| [](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=17f4d6dc73&e=9a7df6c10f)| [](https://BH4C.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=27739a80cfdaa3b90571b5a42&id=1cb0719d85&e=9a7df6c10f)
---|---|---

name: Richard Blumenthal
subject: re: the filibuster
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut
body:
[ ](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=UrVdNfjsA5AOf0t2GcaJlV%2B0dRwzafsv&url=http%3A%2F%2Frichardblumenthal.com%2F)
Friend,
A decade ago now, when I first began my tenure in the Senate, one of my first votes was to eliminate the filibuster. I understood my colleagues' concern - after all, Democrats had spent years in the minority, and they wielded the filibuster as it was institutionally intended: to achieve bipartisan compromise.
But, more recently, I have watched the filibuster bring the Senate to a grinding halt. This institution is no longer functioning as intended. These failures, brought on by the Republican Party's relentless commitment to obstruction, have rendered the filibuster both useless and - much worse - damaging to our country. **[If you believe as strongly as I do that we must get rid of the filibuster, then please add your name to my petition now urging the Senate to eliminate the filibuster for good.](https://petitions.signforgood.com/blumenthal-filibuster?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk1037-su-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Fblumenthal-0521%3Fcode%3Djtk1037-su-nd)**
We are witnessing the damage in this very moment: Right now, there is an unprecedented Republican-led attack on voting rights. From Florida to Michigan to Texas and beyond, extremist legislators are passing laws to purge voter rolls, curb the ability to vote by mail, and even make it illegal to give _water_ to those waiting in line to vote.
The impetus for all of this is crystal clear: feeding into the Big Lie that Donald Trump was cheated out of the election. Despondent from the massive mobilization of Democratic voters last year, Republicans have decided it is easier to suppress voting rights rather than win fair and square. They are masquerading their anti-democratic efforts as "election security."
**We have a legislative solution to all of these attacks right in front of us: passing the For the People Act to protect and expand voting rights - but Republicans are using the filibuster to block its passage.**
This is not the only significant piece of progress that has been blocked because of the filibuster. Increasing numbers of Senators have come around to filibuster reform because its subversion has been used to block things we **_all_** support and need: from funding for VA hospitals to investment in rebuilding America's aging infrastructure. These are the very things where there _should_ be bipartisan action, but there isn't. Because the Republicans don't want to govern all the people. They want government to fail.
When the Senate's functionality comes to a screeching halt - when we can't pass legislation that protects our fundamental right to vote, then we must look to reform. In the face of right-wing, state-based attacks on our democracy, we should be able to pass the For the People Act with just one Republican crossing the aisle to do the right thing. It's what the majority of Americans want us to do. **There 's no hope of any of that unless we eliminate the filibuster. **
**There is unprecedented grassroots momentum for getting rid of the filibuster, and, today, I need 5,000 members of this team to fuel that momentum by signing my petition:**[Will you add your name right now to urge the Senate- including Democratic colleagues who are currently opposed to this action - to get rid of the filibuster once and for all?**** [ADD MY NAME](https://petitions.signforgood.com/blumenthal-filibuster?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk1037-su-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Fblumenthal-0521%3Fcode%3Djtk1037-su-nd) Thus far, not a single Republican Senator has spoken out against the voter suppression tactics happening across the country. In fact, many of my colleagues on the right have _endorsed_ these attacks on the constitutional right to vote. If left unchecked, millions upon millions of voters could be shut out of the ballot box. These attacks are targeted: It's the Americans who turned out for Democrats who will find it more difficult to vote, cementing extremist GOP strongholds on our democracy. You have my word, Friend: I've been fighting with every ounce of energy I have to get the For the People Act passed - and I have hope we can bring at least a few GOP members on board. After all, what could be more important than protecting and expanding the people's ability to vote? It is the most cherished, democratic right in our republic. **But the For the People Act can only get to President Biden 's desk with a simple majority vote. We must eliminate the filibuster. **[So please, sign on to my petition before our 11:59 p.m. deadline urging the Senate to get rid of the filibuster so we can protect voting rights.](https://petitions.signforgood.com/blumenthal-filibuster?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk1037-su-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Fblumenthal-0521%3Fcode%3Djtk1037-su-nd)**** Thank you for taking action on such a crucial issue. Dick [ Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/blumenthal-email?refcode=jtk1037-su-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500) This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from the Blumenthal for Connecticut email list, please click here.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=jV2xcyaQfETs0Dn5N5XxAKTi6hEMS%2BxU&url=http%3A%2F%2Faction.richardblumenthal.com%2Fp%2Fsalsa%2Fsupporter%2Funsubscribe%2Fpublic%2Findex.sjs%3Funsubscribe_page_KEY%3D550%26Email%3D%5B%5BEmail%5D%5D%26email_blast_KEY%3D%5B%5Bemail_blast_KEY%5D%5D) Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut

[](http://www.salsalabs.com/?email)

You have my word, Friend: I've been fighting with every ounce of energy I have to get the For the People Act passed - and I have hope we can bring at least a few GOP members on board. After all, what could be more important than protecting and expanding the people's ability to vote? It is the most cherished, democratic right in our republic. But the For the People Act can only get to President Biden 's desk with a simple majority vote. We must eliminate the filibuster. **** Thank you for taking action on such a crucial issue. Dick This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut

name: Senator Patty Murray
subject: Another exciting year at the Golden Tennis Shoe Awards
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
I love the Golden Tennis Shoe Awards because it’s a chance to recognize people who saw a challenge, knew they could make a difference, and took it on, despite the odds -- and no matter what anyone else had to say. That could not be more true with this year’s awardees.
#
Peter,
Last week, I was thrilled to host the virtual 2021 Golden Tennis Shoe Awards!
I love the Golden Tennis Shoe Awards because it’s a chance to recognize people who saw a challenge, knew they could make a difference, and took it on, despite the odds -- and no matter what anyone else had to say. That could not be more true with this year’s awardees.
Here’s a little bit about each of them -- and a link to a video where you can watch their stories:
**Cheri Tinker** wanted to help homeless veterans in her community with nowhere to turn, so she contacted my office to get help with funding for an emergency veterans shelter, along with transitional housing units to start Sarge's Place Homeless Veteran Program.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069684/291509379/-1963912297?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)
**[Learn more about Cheri’s story on Facebook.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069685/291509380/-1963912297?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)**
**Patricia Whitefoot** , whose sister went missing in 1987, uses her family’s tragic story to raise awareness about the epidemic of missing and murdered Indigenous women.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069686/291509381/559362803?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)
**[Watch Patricia tell her story on Facebook.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069687/291509382/559362803?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)**
**Riddhi Mukhopadhyay** is a champion for survivors of sexual violence in Washington state. Her organization has served hundreds of survivors, especially people of color, in this pandemic.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069688/291509383/-1958899818?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)
**[Learn more on Facebook.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069689/291509384/-1958899818?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)**
I am so proud of all of our awardees. Every year, we get so many extraordinary nominations and it is very difficult to select just a few winners. But I believe that Cheri, Patricia, and Riddhi are setting a wonderful example and representing the very best of Washington state every day.
If you didn’t get the chance to join us, I’ll hope you’ll take a moment to watch the awardees’ videos on **[Facebook](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069690/291509385/-559407511?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)** , **[Twitter](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069691/291509386/1764826855?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)** , or **[Instagram](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069692/291509387/-1690531849?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&emci=76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=13116610)** \-- and then plan to join us in person next year!
Thank you,
Patty

**_As Washington's senior U.S. Senator, Patty Murray is working to help families, solve problems, and defend our progressive values._**
* * *
PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most critical tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can click here to let us know if you would like to [unsubscribe](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/76d2bfe2-86d0-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7/a01b9244-2bd1-eb11-a7ad-501ac57b8fa7?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=&id=115197541). Join us today in fighting for progressive values: [donate now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/31069693/291509388/-1835341818?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDFiOTI0NC0yYmQxLWViMTEtYTdhZC01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BK8miWgR_psJS74ZPeu5mKpmNlmO7pQzaf6T9FhDYh4=).

name: Nicolaus Sleister
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth
body:
We're celebrating Juneteenth!
| [ ](http://www.nick4wa.com/?e=4b51f502eb7221f5033bf92012057078&utm_source=nick4wa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=juneteenth_01&n=1)
---
Peter, Today we celebrate Juneteenth as an official holiday. While this is a step in the right direction, there is still a lot that needs to be done to ensure equity and equality. We still don't have federal police reform; we still don't have education equity, social justice, or action on Climate Change. We cannot stop fighting for the changes that must be made! We celebrate today, but tomorrow we keep fighting. This is progress, but we need bigger and bolder ideas. We need bigger and bolder action. That is what I have been and will continue fighting for! I am running to ensure that everyone has equity and equality in their lives, that every American can reach their dreams. Together we can do this! Please stay safe and enjoy the holiday as we celebrate this historical holiday! Nicolaus Sleister,
[](http://www.nick4wa.com/?e=4b51f502eb7221f5033bf92012057078&utm_source=nick4wa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=juneteenth_01&n=2)[www.Nick4WA.com](https://www.nick4wa.com?e=4b51f502eb7221f5033bf92012057078&utm_source=nick4wa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=juneteenth_01&n=3) [](https://www.nick4wa.com/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6k0Jm8jDE109S7GZfhKfaXmMV0wRNVYoxdZ8n_Nc9D9t&e=4b51f502eb7221f5033bf92012057078&utm_source=nick4wa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=juneteenth_01&n=4)
